attributes.stem: A previously well 28-year-old man presents to his general practitioner with a two month history of hair loss, poor wound healing and a diminished sense of taste. He does not smoke or drink alcohol and has been following a strict plant-based diet for the past two years. The GP suspects a micronutrient deficiency as the cause of his symptoms.
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following deficiencies is most likely responsible for his presentation?
attributes.explanation:
Zinc deficiency can cause hair loss, impaired wound healing and a diminished sense of taste. A strict plant-based diet may result in an inadequate zinc intake, leading to the deficiency and the symptoms the patient is experiencing. Zinc is found in red meat, poultry, seafood and dairy products. Vegans can obtain zinc from legumes, nuts, seeds and whole grains, but may need to pay special attention to their zinc intake due to lower bioavailability from plant sources.

Zinc deficiency can also be associated with skin rashes (that do not improve with topical steroids) and with diarrhoea. In this case, the zinc deficiency is likely due to inadequate intake. However, zinc deficiency can also be inherited (acrodermatitis enteropathica), or due to problems with absorption (e.g., gut diseases), or increased loss of zinc (e.g., diuretics, angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitors, angiotensin receptor blockers).

Iron deficiency can cause hair loss and is associated with fatigue, but it is not typically associated with impaired wound healing or changes in taste perception. Iron is found in red meat, poultry, and fish, but can also be obtained from plant sources such as lentils, beans, and fortified cereals. Vegans may be at a higher risk of iron deficiency due to lower bioavailability of iron from plant sources.

A strict plant-based diet can lead to vitamin B12 deficiency, which is associated with peripheral neuropathy, but this does not typically cause the symptoms of hair loss, poor wound healing, or diminished sense of taste. Vitamin B12 is primarily found in animal products, such as meat, dairy, and eggs. Vegans are at risk for vitamin B12 deficiency and may need to supplement their diet or consume fortified foods.

Vitamin D deficiency can be more common in individuals following a plant-based diet, but it is not typically associated with hair loss, poor wound healing, or changes in taste perception. Vitamin D is found in fatty fish, egg yolks, and fortified dairy products. Vegans can obtain vitamin D through sun exposure, fortified plant-based milks, or supplements.

Vitamin K deficiency is generally not associated with hair loss, poor wound healing, or changes in taste perception, making it an unlikely cause of the patient's symptoms. Vitamin K is found in green leafy vegetables, vegetable oils, and some fruits. Vegans can usually obtain sufficient vitamin K from their diet.
learningPoints: Zinc deficiency can cause hair loss, impaired wound healing, and a diminished sense of taste
references: Zinc deficiency. In: StatPearls [Internet]. Treasure Island (FL): StatPearls Publishing; 2023 Jan-. Available from: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK493231/#:~:text=Zinc%20deficiency%20can%20be%20inherited,results%20in%20quick%20clinical%20improvement.
